output:
CRITICAL - Code Discovery Protocol:
1. ALWAYS use codebase-memory-mcp tools FIRST for ANY code exploration:
   - search_graph(name_pattern/label/qn_pattern) to find functions/classes/routes
   - trace_path(function_name, mode=calls|data_flow|cross_service) for call chains
   - get_code_snippet(qualified_name) to read source (NOT Read/cat)
   - query_graph(query) for complex Cypher patterns
   - get_architecture(aspects) for project structure
   - search_code(pattern) for text search (graph-augmented grep)
2. Fall back to Grep/Glob/Read ONLY for text content, config values, non-code files.
3. If a project is not indexed yet, run index_repository FIRST.

   - search_graph(name_pattern/label/qn_pattern) to find functions/classes/routes

   - get_code_snippet(qualified_name) to read source (NOT Read/cat)
   - query_graph(query) for complex Cypher patterns
   - get_architecture(aspects) for project structure
   - search_code(pattern) for text search (graph-augmented grep)
2. Fall back to Grep/Glob/Read ONLY for text content, config values, non-code files.
3. If a project is not indexed yet, run index_repository FIRST.

input.command:
for f in classify_test.go gc_test.go hierarchical_bench_test.go hierarchical_test.go manager_test.go proof_test.go strata_parity_test.go verify_test.go; do
  echo "=== $f ==="
  grep -E "^func Test|^func Bench" /Users/dayna.blackwell/code/knowing/internal/snapshot/$f
done

content.text:
Here are the test files in `internal/snapshot/` and what they cover:

| File | Coverage |
|---|---|
| `classify_test.go` | Change classification logic: no-change, nil, behavioral vs structural vs metadata-only changes, edge type handling, precedence rules |
| `gc_test.go` | Garbage collection: stats population, zero-stats when nothing is pruned |
| `hierarchical_bench_test.go` | Performance benchmarks for flat diff at 10K/50K/100K nodes |
| `hierarchical_test.go` | Hierarchical Merkle tree: build (empty, deterministic, order-independence, package/edge-type roots), diff (identical, added/removed/changed packages), filtering and max-changes options |
| `manager_test.go` | Core snapshot manager: Merkle root determinism, diff identification, snapshot chaining, GC chain integrity, package path extraction, malformed node skipping |
| `proof_test.go` | Merkle inclusion/absence proofs: generate+verify, tamper detection (edge and sibling), edge/package not found, many-edge cases, proof size |
| `strata_parity_test.go` | Strata-level parity check (single test: `TestStrataParity`) |
| `verify_test.go` | Graph verification: clean repo, dangling edges, broken chains |

There's no separate `merkle_test.go` — the Merkle hash logic is tested indirectly through `manager_test.go` and `proof_test.go`. The `merkle.go` file itself has no dedicated test file.

| File | Coverage |
|---|---|
| `classify_test.go` | Change classification logic: no-change, nil, behavioral vs structural vs metadata-only changes, edge type handling, precedence rules |
| `gc_test.go` | Garbage collection: stats population, zero-stats when nothing is pruned |
| `hierarchical_bench_test.go` | Performance benchmarks for flat diff at 10K/50K/100K nodes |
| `hierarchical_test.go` | Hierarchical Merkle tree: build (empty, deterministic, order-independence, package/edge-type roots), diff (identical, added/removed/changed packages), filtering and max-changes options |
| `manager_test.go` | Core snapshot manager: Merkle root determinism, diff identification, snapshot chaining, GC chain integrity, package path extraction, malformed node skipping |
| `proof_test.go` | Merkle inclusion/absence proofs: generate+verify, tamper detection (edge and sibling), edge/package not found, many-edge cases, proof size |
| `strata_parity_test.go` | Strata-level parity check (single test: `TestStrataParity`) |
| `verify_test.go` | Graph verification: clean repo, dangling edges, broken chains |

There's no separate `merkle_test.go` — the Merkle hash logic is tested indirectly through `manager_test.go` and `proof_test.go`. The `merkle.go` file itself has no dedicated test file.
